ocr.text: The telecommunications landscape in the Asia-Pacific region continues to evolve rapidly, driven by increasing demand for
high-speed internet services and innovative connectivity solutions. As outlined in the Subscriber Growth by Segment and Fiber-
to-the-Home Deployment data, there has been a notable shift towards Fixed Wireless technologies, with subscriber growth
indicating a substantial rise.
Segment  Amount($M)  YoYChange(%)  MarketShare(%)  TotalSubscribers  AverageRevenue($)
Broadband  1500  8.0  25.0  0.8M  TotalGrowth
FixedWireless  600  10.0  10.0  0.3M  2000  IoT
[HIDDEN]  12.5  5.0  [HIDDEN]  4000  OverallTotal  5000
6.0  100.0  3.8M  1300  [HIDDEN]  3.5  ProjectedGrowth
Specifically, the segment saw an increase in subscribers to 400, reflecting the overarching trend towards greater reliance on
wireless connectivity. Additionally, the demand for IoT services is projected to boost overall subscriber numbers, with
IncreasedDemand capturing the attention of service providers.
FinancialOverview  RevenueDetails  InvestmentMetrics
Quarter  Revenue(SGD)  GrowthRate(%)  CAPEX(SGD)  NetIncome(SGD)  EBITDA(SGD)  SubscriberBase  ChurnRate(%)
510M  6.3  135M  210M  290M  5.0M  1.9  YearlyGrowth(%)  8.0  InvestmentGrowth(%)  10.0
[HIDDEN]  85.0  AverageChurnRate  2.0  TotalCAPEX  135M  TotalSubscribers  1.4M  TotalEBITDA  290M  EndofPeriod
The metrics reveal that the overall total of subscribers reached 2000, showcasing the competitive environment of the
telecommunications market. In terms of future projections, the average revenue per subscriber is anticipated to align with trends
observed in Q12025, where the AverageRevenue/Subscriber was reported as 1.9. Furthermore, the growth trajectory suggests
that there could be an increase of 0.1M in the Fixed Wireless segment, emphasizing the potential for future expansion. As
telecommunications companies navigate these dynamics, understanding these metrics will be crucial in strategizing for
sustainable growth and enhancing service offerings to meet consumer needs.
